serving.description: 1 full packet (105g, prepared with all sachets, soup-style)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one full packet (105g dry weight) prepared soup-style with all included sachets — flavouring powder, coconut milk powder, chilli paste, and belacan — yielding approximately 475 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: MyKuali Penang White Curry Noodle — Nutrition Information Panel (Singapore-sold product label)
nutrient_source.organization: MyKuali Food Industries Sdn Bhd — Singapore-sold product NIP
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the Nutrition Information Panel on the Singapore-sold MyKuali Penang White Curry Noodle product label (105g per packet). This is a USDA Branded Food-equivalent product label source. Values are per 100g of prepared product as declared on pack. Approved under Project Veritas source policy as a verified product NIP for a packaged commercial food.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Instant Noodles — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (instant noodle category, GI ~47–52)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for MyKuali Penang White Curry Noodle specifically. GI ~50 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entries for equivalent instant noodle products (wheat-based, boiled, GI range 47–52). The high fat content from coconut milk (14.3g saturated fat per 100g) is expected to moderately suppress postprandial glucose response. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 59.96) / 100 ≈ 30.0. Carbohydrate per serving = 57.1g/100g × 105g = 59.96g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1,143mg per 100g; approximately 1,200mg per full packet (105g) — equivalent to 60% of the WHO 2,000mg/day recommended limit in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit MyKuali Penang White Curry Noodle; one packet delivers ~1,200mg sodium (60% of daily limit), and consuming the full soup broth further increases sodium intake beyond the declared NIP value.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~50 (low-medium) but glycemic load ~30 per packet is high due to large serving size (105g); energy-dense at 475 kcal per packet with 15g saturated fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes may consume this product occasionally given its moderate GI (~50), but the high glycemic load (~30 per packet), energy density (475 kcal), and very high saturated fat (15g) make it unsuitable for regular consumption, particularly for those managing weight or cardiovascular risk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 14.3g per 100g (15g per packet) — approximately 75% of the 20g/day RDI — predominantly from coconut milk lauric acid; combined with 1,200mg sodium per packet
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat this product as an occasional food only; the combination of very high saturated fat (~15g per serving, 75% of daily RDI) from coconut milk and high sodium (~1,200mg per serving) presents a significant cumulative cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very high saturated fat (15g per packet) and high sodium (1,200mg per packet) exacerbate cardiovascular risk and NSAID-related fluid retention in RA patients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption; the very high saturated fat load (15g per serving) elevates cardiovascular risk already elevated in RA, and high sodium worsens NSAID-related fluid retention.

llm_training.natural_language_description: MyKuali Penang White Curry Noodle is a premium Malaysian-origin instant noodle product sold widely in Singapore supermarkets (Cold Storage, FairPrice Finest) and online. It was voted the world's best instant noodle by The Ramen Rater in 2014 and became a viral collectible product, with Singaporeans gifting packs internationally. The product reproduces the flavour profile of Penang-style curry mee — a rich, aromatic white coconut milk-based curry noodle soup — in a convenient packet format. One packet (105g dry weight) is prepared by boiling the noodles and combining with all included sachets: a flavouring powder, a coconut milk powder sachet, a chilli paste sachet, and a belacan (fermented shrimp paste) sachet. The resulting soup-style dish is intensely flavourful, creamy, mildly spicy, and aromatic with lemongrass and galangal notes. Nutritionally, this product is significantly different from standard instant noodles: the coconut milk powder sachet contributes approximately 15g of saturated fat per packet (predominantly lauric acid), making it one of the highest saturated fat instant noodle products in Singapore's market. Sodium per packet is approximately 1,200mg — among the highest in the Singapore instant noodle category and equivalent to 60% of the WHO daily sodium limit in a single meal. Energy per packet is approximately 475 kcal. GI is estimated at ~50 (low-medium) based on analogous wheat-based instant noodle data from the University of Sydney GI Database, but glycemic load per packet is high (~30) due to the large serving size. Clinically, this is best treated as a special-occasion food for patients managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes. The product is halal-certified and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore-sold product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) on the MyKuali Penang White Curry Noodle packet — an exact product label match. All core macros and secondary nutrients are declared on-pack. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (instant noodle category) with documented estimation basis.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not declared on the Singapore-sold product NIP and is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database for this specific branded product as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content of the prepared product is not declared on the NIP and is not available in approved sources for this specific product. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. GI value of 50 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for equivalent wheat-based instant noodle products (GI range 47–52); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific branded product. GL per serving calculated as (50 × 59.96) / 100 = 30.0, where carbohydrate per serving = 57.1g/100g × 105g = 59.96g. The product is sold as a dry packet (105g); the per_100g values reflect the prepared product as declared on the Singapore-sold NIP. Sodium per packet ≈ 1,200mg (1,143mg/100g × 1.05). Saturated fat per packet ≈ 15g (14.3g/100g × 1.05). Energy per packet ≈ 475 kcal (452 kcal/100g × 1.05).
